does: SALSA proposes a method to improve RLHF by replacing the frozen reference policy with a model soup created through weight-space averaging of two independent supervised fine-tuned models. Instead of using a single initial checkpoint as the KL constraint reference in PPO, the approach generates a more flexible reference that permits larger KL divergence exploration. The paper demonstrates that this averaged reference model enables better exploration of the reward landscape, allowing policy optimization to discover higher-quality solutions while maintaining alignment with human preferences. The core innovation lies in using model soup to create a better-positioned reference point that balances constraint enforcement with improved solution quality.
connects: This work builds on the established RLHF pipeline, particularly extending PPO-based alignment methods that use KL divergence penalties to constrain policy drift from initialization. The research responds to known limitations of frozen reference models in policy optimization and draws from recent work on model merging and weight-space averaging, which has shown promise in combining diverse model capabilities. The paper connects to broader efforts in improving exploration-exploitation tradeoffs in reinforcement learning and addresses practical challenges in LLM alignment where overly restrictive reference constraints limit adaptation quality. It extends beyond standard supervised fine-tuning practices by leveraging multiple SFT checkpoints to construct a superior reference policy.
enables: SALSA opens opportunities for more flexible constraint mechanisms in RLHF and suggests broader applications of model soup techniques in policy optimization beyond language models. The approach points toward further research on optimal reference policy selection and weight-averaging strategies for multi-objective optimization in alignment. It could enable stronger adaptation across diverse reward signals and reward specifications, potentially improving final model quality in practical LLM deployment. Follow-up work might explore ensemble-based references, online model soup construction, and applications to multi-task alignment where different reference models capture distinct aspects of human preferences worth preserving during policy adaptation.
